assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Explicitly states 'Each TPU slice (256 chips) is recreated entirely in a single step — not rolling upgrades like CPU/GPU nodes' and emphasizes atomic recreation

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Clearly states 'Any maintenance event on ANY of your 4 slices triggers restart of ALL slices' and 'if GKE touches just one slice, your entire 1,024-chip training environment goes down'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: States 'Complete Training Interruption: Your LLM training will be fully interrupted' and 'All pods on each slice restart simultaneously, losing any in-memory state'

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Explicitly states 'If insufficient TPU v5e capacity exists in your zone during recreation, slices will block until hardware becomes available' and mentions downtime could extend to several hours

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Includes 'Force Checkpoint Save' section with specific commands and emphasizes 'Checkpoint saved at latest training step' in the checklist

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: States 'Training script handles checkpoint resume correctly' in checklist and provides 'Resume Training' section with checkpoint loading verification

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Provides detailed gcloud command for maintenance exclusions and suggests using them 'during critical training phases'

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: References 'Maintenance window timing confirmed with GKE' in checklist and 'BEFORE maintenance window' in preparation steps
